text_source: Hm...
Urgh. Toilet. Gotta go...
...Phew.
Gah, that'll teach me to drink tea before bed.\n
It's cold out here.
*CLATTER*
Hm?
Halfway back to my room from the toilet, a sound\n
from the inn's main entrance distracts me.
Curious to know what could be making such noise\n
at this hour, I decide to have a peek...
...Kuon?
From a distance, I can distinguish the shape\n
of Kuon's back as she slips outside.
What's she doing, leaving the inn this late?\n
Isn't it dangerous to go out alone? What if\n
something...?
Nah, I shouldn't worry. Kuon can take care of\n
herself, anyway.
Ah... Sleepy.
Well, whatever. Maybe she just wanted to go look\n
at the moon and get some fresh air or something.
As long as I'm out here, I guess I'll have a\n
drink and stargaze before I go back to bed...
...I'm bored.
It's one of those rare days where, for once,\n
no crisis needs my attention. A free\n
afternoon...
Everyone seems to be out of the inn, too.
Kuon went out with Nekone and Rulutieh.\n
They did invite me, but I'm not up for being\n
a pack mule.
Kiwru seemed perfectly happy to go carry stuff\n
for them in my stead, anyway...
Gotta wonder if being treated so dismissively\n
is that guy's kink or something.
Now that I think about it...
I take a sip of tea, ruminating.
We've been calling this place our\n
"headquarters," but I don't really know much\n
about the Hakurokaku.
I guess this is my opportunity to explore the\n
place a bit. And who knows? I might find\n
something.
With my mind made up, I venture out of my room\n
and into the hallway.
Huh, it's a lot more spacious back here than\n
I'd have thought...
And all these stairs... It's like a labyrinth\n
with multiple floors.
Well, I guess I'll explore the upper floors,\n
since I haven't been up there at all yet.
The stairs all lead to different places, too.\n
Hope I don't get lost. Why would they design it\n
like this?
I wander around the upper floors of the\n
Hakurokaku, occasionally passing workers\n
cleaning the rooms.
Walking around like this is giving me a sense\n
for how strangely laid-out this building is...
What's this place...?
Finally, after a long ascent, I emerge into a\n
spacious room with no more stairs that lead\n
upward.
Can't go up any more. Is this the top floor...?
The room's wide windows lie open to the outside.\n
An observatory of some kind...?
Wow, that view... I think I can see the\n
whole imperial capital from here.
The patterns of greenery in the city's gardens\n
are a sight to behold from above like this.
Hm. Kuon might appreciate this place.\n
Guess I'll let her know about it whenever\n
she gets back.
Ah... What a nice day, though.
A warm breeze filters in through the open\n
windows, and I stretch my arms with a long\n
sigh.
Not a single cloud mars the perfection of the\n
blue sky, and the sun makes the city sparkle.
A nice, cold drink to enjoy under the sun would\n
be great on a day like this...
Might as well go and get something to drink,\n
since I found this place to savor it in.
With Kuon and the others gone, it isn't like\n
I'm going to get told off for day-drinking.
Guess I'll head d--huh?
Just as I turn to go back to my room, my eyes\n
fall on a strange pattern on the wall.
This pattern...
Upon closer inspection, it appears to be made\n
of multiple wooden, interlocking square pieces.
And it looks... decidedly jumbled. All over\n
the place.
Are the pieces in the wrong order, maybe?\n
I could swear this piece goes here...
*CLACK*
It moves?
When I touch a piece of the apparent puzzle,\n
it slides ever-so-slightly to the side.
Does that mean...?
*CLACK, CLICK*
If this goes here, and that slides into place\n
down there...
Is it really a puzzle, then? My curiosity gets\n
the better of me, and I begin to move the\n
pieces.
*CLICK, CLACK*...
Gradually, the pieces begin to form a proper\n
geometric pattern, coaxed out of their\n
disarray.
And that should do it...
*CLUNK*
*Rumble, rumble*...
Wh-
As the last piece slots into its place, some\n
mechanism triggers, and a staircase slides\n
into view.
A staircase... So there's another floor above\n
this one?
This is quite an elaborate setup.\n
Now I HAVE to see what's up there.
I hesitate only a moment, failing to fight\n
down my curiosity as I ascend the revealed\n
steps.
A faintly sweet-smelling fragrance wafts down\n
the stairs...
Hm? Ah, that... That smells nice. Some kind of\n
incense?
This is...
The stairs emerge into a room filled with the\n
smoke of the mysterious incense.
Though dark, my eyes quickly grow accustomed,\n
and I can tell the room is furnished lavishly.
I thought I would find an unused attic or\n
something up here, but...
...Maybe I shouldn't have come up here without\n
permission.
Well, now. Do I have a visitor?
Bwuh!?
In a corner of the room draped with furs,\n
a beautiful, elegantly-dressed woman reclines\n
on a couch.
She has an alluring, graceful air about her,\n
coiled and composed like a self-assured\n
predator.
The sight is enough to take me off guard,\n
enamored for a fleeting moment. She sips her\n
drink.
That looks good, whatever it is.\n
Wish I had a cup.
Lady
Did you have some business here?
O-Oh, uh, no. Sorry for entering without\n
I'm not up to anything shady, promise--I just\n
sort of... wandered in here. I'll excuse\n
myself now.
Mm. Hm hm. There's no need to be in such a\n
hurry.
Halfway to the door, the woman's amused laugh\n
gives me pause.
How about this instead?
She tosses something toward me.
I almost drop it in my surprise, but somehow\n
manage to recover and maintain a good grip.
Um... A cup?
There are rules to this place, you know.\n
Any who enter must partake in at least one\n
drink with me.
You have entered, and so the rules must be\n
observed.
...Huh?
What is this awesome rule? Drinking in the\n
middle of the day? Outrageous, surely.
Of course, I don't want to break the rules,\n
so I GUESS I have no choice. I'll just have\n
to drink.
I quickly step over to the couch and avail\n
myself of an odd-shaped bottle nearby.
Oh, come now. I'm not a barbarian. It would be\n
uncouth to make a guest pour his own drink.
The woman takes the bottle from my hands,\n
tilting it toward me in offering.
Shall we start with just the one cup, then?
...Whoa, whoa.
Her graceful, honest air catches me off-guard\n
once again, and my cup nearly overflows.
Welp. Without further ado...
I bring the cup to my lips and drink deeply.
Ahh... That's the good stuff.
Smooth, rich flavors, faintly sweet, and\n
refreshing brightness, to say nothing of the\n
scent...
Whew. This, truly, is the meaning of enjoying\n
something to its fullest.
I wonder what kind of alcohol this is...?
I would say I've never tasted anything like it,\n
but it's a... nostalgic flavor?
I've definitely had this before, but where...?
What is this?
Mm, you like it? It's nothing less than the\n
crown jewel of my reserves. A masterpiece.
It's foreign in origin, of course, so it may\n
taste strange to you.
Foreign?
I almost remember something, but the alcohol\n
slowly begins to take effect, and the memory\n
evaporates.
Is something the matter?
It's nothing, just...
I think I've had this somewhere before.
I don't really remember it, but it's...\n
just a feeling.
...I see.
The woman gives a small, quiet smile, then\n
gracefully tilts her cup back and drains it.
After seeing that, I pick up the bottle and offer\n
it towards her empty cup.
Hmhm...
I was curious as to what kind of man you are,\n
you know. I'm pleased to find you good drinking\n
company.
Huh? You know who I am?
Nothing that goes on in this inn escapes my\n
notice. You've been the talk of my employees,\n
you know.
...Talk of your employees?
What does she mean by that?
To hear them talk, it seems a man of great\n
wealth has moved in, said to be especially fond\n
of baths...
They also whisper of a menacing nugwisomkami\n
that threatens to devour the kitchen's whole\n
larder.
Wait, wait, hold on. That's definitely my\n
partner they're talking about, not me.
I genuinely had no idea rumors like that were\n
spreading among the staff.
I guess it's only natural, when you consider\n
her appetite and the time she spends bathing...
Ah, is that so? Then you're her companion,\n
Or?
Mm, nothing.
...I don't know why, but now I'm really curious\n
as to what she was going to say.
Maybe it's a little late to be asking this now,\n
but... just who is this person?
Judging by the luxurious room, the hidden\n
entryway, the fact that it's on the top\n
floor...
The unusual architecture's to the owner's\n
personal taste, apparently.
Just between us, the proprietress is a drop-dead\n
gorgeous woman. She's got crazy strength, too.
This woman must be the owner of the Hakurokaku\n
Inn.
That she said "my employees" and knows about\n
everything in the inn only makes me more sure\n
of that.
What bugs me more are the vibes I keep getting\n
off of her...
She reminds me of someone, but who...?
Is there something on my face?
Oh... S-Sorry, no.
Crap. I was staring, wasn't I?
Ah, but how rude of me. I've yet to introduce\n
myself. You may call me Karulau, if it pleases\n
you.
Karulau... I guess it was my imagination.\n
I don't know anyone by that name.
I go by Haku.
...Haku?
That's... quite a rare name, these days.
Yeah, well. It sounds pretty simple and\n
bland to me.
It's not a name from around here, that much is\n
certain--and parents avoid using it in its\n
homeland.
...What do you mean?
It's the name of a great, beloved owlo in that\n
country. A household name, one even children\n
respect.
It would be as if a Yamatan parent named their\n
child after the Mikado himself. A name beyond\n
station.
Now that I think about it, Kuon did say she\n
got my name from a famous person...
Wonder why she decided on it.
That girl... gave you that name, did she?
Yeah. When she found me, I was an amnesiac.\n
Having no name would be inconvenient, so she\n
gave me one.
...I guess there isn't really any reason to\n
hide that. It won't hurt to tell her.
Is that... so? Your memories...\n
That must be very difficult for you.
Well, I guess...
I mean, the first thing I remember is almost\n
getting eaten by a gigiri and a Tatari, in that\n
order.
I thought I was saved, but then I was forced\n
to walk until my legs seized with pain...
I don't know how many times I said I couldn't\n
go on any more.
It appears you've endured quite an ordeal,\n
then.
You seem to have led a rather amu--\n
Ahem, eventful life. Would you be opposed to\n
sharing more details?
Being pent up in a place like this all the time,\n
it's hard to come by entert--ah, stories of the\n
world.
...The way she says it kinda bugs me,\n
but whatever. Sure.
...And after we dispatched the gigiri, we\n
decided to head for the capital together--\n
whoop, that's enough...
Karulau nods along with my story, refilling my\n
cup with impeccable timing as it runs empty.
Through the whole retelling of the gigiri hunt,\n
she's been wordlessly keeping me topped off...
And on the road to the capital, of course, we\n
end up fighting a group of bandits and their\n
chief.
Seriously, why does this stuff keep happening\n
to me?
I have a feeling you're just the sort of man\n
who attracts danger.
It's a rare fate, but not unheard of. Some\n
people are born under odd stars, destined to\n
lead odd lives.
Stars? Fate? Not sure if I put any stock in\n
all that...
You believe such things are absurdities, don't\n
you?
But sometimes they're all we have to explain\n
the inexplicable. I'm sure you know all about\n
it, hm?
Urgh. Give me a break.
Although... Now that she frames it like that,\n
maybe fate IS to blame for how my life's been\n
going.
Of course, it's not all bad. Poor fortune isn't\n
all that's attracted to you, is it?
It's also been facilitating meetings, putting\n
you in the paths of those you now hold dear...
An enviable quality, to be sure.\n
And a rare one.
My cup fills again just as quickly as it\n
emptied.
Yooouuuu really think sho...?
Urk. Alcohol's getting to me.\n
I can't seem to form my words properly...
Sssho, where did I leave off...?
Ahh... Mmrgnh. Sleepy... Maybe I got carried\n
away with the expensive booze.
Is something the matter? You've stopped\n
drinking.
Oh... right...
Oh, my. Asleep already, are we?
Hm hm. Such an innocent expression when you\n
sleep...
Yes...
That gift you hold is a rare one indeed.
The same gift, I think, as the man you take\n
your name from...
It can only be fate that led you to meet.\n
Yes, you were destined to cross one another's\n
paths...
Everything in its proper place and time,\n
predetermined by providence.
What paths does fate hold for you from here on,\n
I wonder...?
Will you become a conqueror, plunging the land\n
into turmoil as you recklessly make your wars?
Or... perhaps you will shoulder the burdens of\n
the many, and rule with kindness and love.
I thought I could hear a voice...
It tickled my ears, whispering pleasantly.
It... felt soft. Like Kuon's tail.
Oh... That was it. That's... who she reminded\n
me of.
Kuon...
When I awaken, I find myself in my own bed.
...Was all that just a dream?
A familiar, oddly-shaped bottle sitting next\n
to the water pitcher begs to differ.
Late at night...
Kiwru and I make our patrol of the city,\n
keeping a wary eye out for fires and suspicious\n
figures.
Gah, but it's cold... Working for free was\n
never my cup of tea, especially in this chill.
But this is our duty! It falls to us to help\n
maintain the public peace.
You're earnest to a fault, y'know. Try saying\n
that to the others, in their warm beds back\n
at the inn.
It's no coincidence Kuon and the others\n
assigned this job to us, conveniently\n
exempting themselves.
Under the pretext of "sharing the workload,"\n
we've been stuck with patrols and they get\n
beauty rest.
And with this weather... Sheesh, we've really\n
drawn the short straw on this one.
It certainly is chilly. I just hope none of\n
the usual drunks pass out in the cold...
I could go for a drink, myself. I would've\n
brought some sake, had I known about the\n
weather.
You shouldn't patrol drunk! What state would\n
that leave you in if something needs your\n
attention?
Kiwru smiles wryly as he speaks.
Well, think of it this way. The cold impedes\n
how effectively we patrol, right?
Wouldn't it make us more efficient peacekeepers\n
if we had a drink to warm us up and raise our\n
spirits?
Eh...
So, in light of that reasoning, you should\n
lend me some cash.
What? Why?
They made me leave my wallet behind.\n
It's not like I had a choice.
Haku...
Don't give me that pitying look.\n
Come on.
All right. I'm a little hungry myself, so if\n
it's only a light drink... Let's find a place.
...Y'know what, never mind.
Huh?
Nngh. I know I'm the one who suggested it,\n
but now I just feel pathetic.
Let's keep patrolling, then.
In the end, the cold continues to relentlessly\n
bite...
Just then, something catches my eye--a straw\n
mat, left out to dry and forgotten.
Well, now. There's an idea...
Um, Haku? What are you going to do with that?
What? Waste not, want not. It's just common\n
wisdom.
I wrap the borrowed mat about my shoulders\n
like a cloak...
Phew, this'll insulate me a bit.
Hey, don't look at me like that. I'm freezing\n
half to death over here.
At this rate, YOU'RE the one who's going to\n
attract suspicion dressed like that.
What are you talking about? A true gentleman\n
can make anything work for his outfit, if worn\n
stylishly.
...I give up. I can't keep up with your\n
extremely positive thinking.
You should work on that, then. Practice\n
thoughts like that every day, if you can.
Right...
Hm?
Stop right there! You're under arrest!
...What's all this?
Sounds like bandits. It came from over there...\n
We should go assist.
No, we shouldn't. Everyone has their assigned\n
patrol route. We need to stick to ours.
But...
What if we leave our post and something happens\n
here without us? We can't move around\n
carelessly.
You're... You're right. We'd best patrol the\n
area we're assigned. It would be imprudent to\n
abandon it.
That's right. I'm not trying to weasel out of\n
something tiresome--it's just the safer play.
Then, all at once, a figure seems to materialize\n
from nothing as it bolts toward us around the\n
corner.
Whoa--
Gh--Who are you!?
Girl
I'm sorry, but could you help me out? I'm being\n
chased by some awfully unpleasant men.
Going by her voice, she sounds to be a woman--\n
but she hides in the shadows before I can see\n
her face.
Capital guard
You there! Did anyone suspicious pass through\n
here just now?
A group of capital guards appears in short\n
order, tromping through the streets with heavy\n
footsteps.
She said she was being chased by "unpleasant\n
men," but it's these guys, huh...?
They're definitely capital guards, not ruffians.
Which means... I should turn her in to them,\n
right? Or... No, wait.
I only caught a momentary glimpse of her, but\n
I'm fairly sure I've seen her before. She's...\n
that...
I see. If that's the case...
Um, well--
Eh, no one unusual. Why? Did something happen?
Huh...?
We're tracking a suspect spotted fleeing the\n
scene of a storehouse robbery. Have you seen\n
anything?
Now that you mention it, I did hear pretty\n
frantic footsteps over that way. That's all,\n
though.
That's probably our thief. There's no mistaking\n
it, right?
Mm, well. To the best of my memory, they took\n
off down that alleyway there.
All right, then we'll pursue. Thank you for\n
your assistance, citizen.
The guard turns to run in the direction I\n
indicated, then pauses and turns back to me.
Oh, and citizen? If you've no work, you should\n
visit the job placement office when you can.
The guard avoids looking at the straw mat\n
wrapped around me.
I know you've got stuff to say, but I have my\n
reasons.
I don't think the mat was a good idea.\n
I-I think they mistook you for a beggar...
...Yeah, you're right.
Thank you. You both did wonderfully.
As the guards' booted footfalls grow more and\n
more distant, the girl peeks out of her cover,\n
wary.
I should thank you properly. I'll never forget\n
this favor.
I thought I'd seen her face once before.\n
Her name was... Nosuri, that's it.
I made the right choice after all. Handing\n
someone working with us to the guards would've\n
ended badly.
Don't worry about it. I had my own reasons.
Reasons?
By "reasons," surely you don't mean...\n
You know who I am...?
Well, you could say that.
I see. You recognized me and hoped to preserve\n
my freedom... All my hard work hasn't been for\n
naught.
Misunderstanding, Nosuri looks at me with\n
grateful eyes, moved to the point of her voice\n
wavering.
You'd best get out of here before too long.\n
They'll be back.
Yes, I'll be away shortly. But you...
Hm. So even in the affluence of the capital,\n
there are those who live in the shadows...
My mission hasn't been in error, after all...!
To live so honorably, even as a poor man...\n
For the sake of people like you, I will strive\n
yet harder.
Nosuri sheds a dramatic tear, then produces a\n
small bag from her pocket.
Here, friend. Use this to eat something warm\n
on this cold night.
This is...
The bag feels heavy for some reason.
Don't say anything, sir. I know. Even in the\n
Mikado's own city, the destitute of the world\n
endure...
Try not to catch a cold in this weather, sir.\n
Be well!
Then, with surprising dexterity, Nosuri nimbly\n
bounds off into the night.
What's with the bag?
No idea. Feels heavy.
I loosen the bag's drawstrings to find... money.\n
A LOT of money. The bag is stuffed to the brim.
...Um. So she was a bandit, right? A fugitive?\n
Is it OK that we just let her off the hook?
Yeah. I've got my reasons.
Oshtor's aware of her, so it ought to be just\n
fine.
B-Brother knows of her...? That's OK, then,\n
but what are you going to do with that?
I regard the bag lying open in my hands.\n
It's... really not a small amount. This is a\n
fat wad of cash.
Isn't that... the money stolen in the robbery?
I doubt it would look good if we have that\n
when the guards come back...
...In that case, I'll leave it in your care,\n
Kiwru.
Huh? N-No! I can't be seen with that; I'll be\n
arrested!
Well, it's not like I can keep it, either!\n
Just hold onto it for now.
Don't be unreasonable! Y-You do something\n
about it.
Urgh--
Nngh--!
The coin pouch jingles as we toss it back and\n
forth frantically, trying to force it on each\n
other.
Uh--
Wh--Uh oh.
*Toss*...
As our struggle continues, someone--I'm not\n
sure who--fumbles the bag, and it sails off\n
into the dark.
Well! We've got a patrol to get back to.
...Yeah, let's finish our patrol.
Alack, how the cold doth bore into my very\n
bones! To say nothing of the late hour...
Upon so chill a night as this, a drink to warm\n
the spirit could--Nay, thrift. I must be\n
thrifty.
GYAH!? Pain! Whence doth this pain come?
Maroro suddenly flinches, writhing in pain as\n
a blunt object takes him off-guard.
Wh-Who? Who would hold such cruelty and\n
discontent for poor Maroro as to throw stones\n
at him?
...Alas, no voice doth answer. But how now!\n
What's this?
Wh-Why--such lucre! Wealth beyond compare!\n
A great hoard of coin, in so plain a vessel...
Praise the day, blessed coin, for thou art\n
sufficient to pay Maroro's debts for once and\n
always!
Oh, a prescient blessing thou art.\n
Heaven doth smile sweetly upon poor Maroro\n
this day, forsooth!
Whence this blessing cometh, so too doth good\n
fortune fall at last as a mantle o'er my\n
shoulders.
Ah, you there. A moment, sir.
What ho?
I want you to tell me where you got that\n
money from.
Wh-Wherefore dost thou ask? This coin hath\n
bestowed itself upon me, as befits a gift from\n
on high.
...Very well. I'll hear all the details at the\n
garrison.
Wh--Stay thy hands! I told thee, this money is\n
the heavens' own blessing upon me!
All right, let's take him away.
Dragged by his arms, Maroro is taken away to\n
the capital guards' garrison.
I've done naught wrong! 'Tis a blessing from\n
the heavens, I say! A blessing...!
The scholar's cries fall on deaf ears as the\n
faint light of dawn begins to creep into the\n
sky.
Kid, you around?
In the middle of a perfectly good afternoon\n
of lazing around at our headquarters, Ukon\n
pops in.
What, another job already? I'd prefer if you\n
saved that stuff for when Kuon is around.
Nah, that's not it. I've got some business\n
with you, kid. Come tag along with me for\n
awhile, huh?
Y'know, whenever you make a roundabout\n
invitation like that, it typically ends with\n
me in trouble.
Come on, don't be like that. I was just gonna\n
go for a little walk, maybe look into one or\n
two things.
So, how about it? You coming with?
I may be putting my work as Ukon in your guys'\n
hands, but I'm not about to stop patrolling\n
the city.
Man, I was gonna take a nap while everyone's\n
out...
Bah, it's nothing big. We'll walk around a bit,\n
grab a drink on the way back. My treat, of\n
course.
His treat...?
Well, if it's just patrolling the area...\n
I mean, it is JUST patrolling the area, right?
Yeah, that's all. Promise.
Ukon gives his answer with a nonchalant grin.
All right, all right. How can I say no to you?\n
Let's get going.
Knew you'd come around, kid.
Hurry up and get ready. We'll talk on the way.
Got it.
So, when you say "contraband," you mean...?
Hey, sshh. Not so loud, kid.
But yeah. Word is they've been sneaking stuff\n
through here, so we're gonna find some answers.
What? You said we were just gonna be patrolling.
Yeah, and we're doing this while we patrol.\n
See? I didn't lie about anything.
You jerk.
Anyway!
To be straight with you, I don't even know\n
what the contraband item actually is.
Heard it was "live" and "fresh," but that\n
could mean anything.
Live and fresh... Some kind of food, maybe.
Or pleasure-seekers smuggling in something\n
psychoactive. Herbs or mushrooms of some kind?
All right, I get the gist. Where do I figure\n
into it?
Nah, you're good. I just want you to get\n
familiar with the faces of folks who run in\n
these circles.
Just act like you're my buddy. Smile, nod, look\n
like you're supposed to be here.
Gotta say, though, I'm glad you were the one\n
at headquarters.
Walking around with one of the princesses...\n
We'd stick out like big, throbbing sore\n
thumbs.
So you chose me because...
I'm not trying to say you look like a bum.\n
You've got a good... I dunno, like a friendly\n
attitude.
And you're not bad-looking. You've got a face\n
people wanna trust.
I'm not sure if you're insulting me,\n
complimenting me, or both.
As requested, I stick close to Ukon and follow\n
him through the city after that.
Ukon's popularity with the city's commonfolk\n
becomes more and more apparent as we walk\n
downtown.
Townsfolk
Ah! If it isn't Ukon. Long time no see!
Master Ukon! I haven't thanked you for all you\n
did for me the other day...
Hey, look! Ukon's here!
Ukon, come play with us!
I've just finished off a fresh batch of mamutu.\n
Here, don't be shy--take one.
Oh, have some dango with it, as well!\n
Here, this one's fresh.
Really, now? Thanks. I'll help myself, if you\n
insist.
Why don't you rest your heels here awhile?\n
I'll put the tea on.
Thanks for the offer, but I'm a little bit\n
busy. This here's my friend from the\n
countryside, see.
He just arrived in the capital, so I'm showing\n
him around the place. Can't stop to chat right\n
now.
Ah, that's a shame. Maybe next time.
I'll drop by for tea later. For right now,\n
though... You guys hear anything lately?
The kid's been asking if I've got any good\n
stories. Y'know, strange stuff that only\n
happens here.
That's how he's doing this, huh? Guess I can\n
play my part...
Yeah, I can't get enough of those. I was hoping\n
for more juicy gossip about the capital, but\n
Ukon's out.
Bahaha! Well, I've been away for a long time\n
...Heard anything, eh...?
Well, this place's nice and quiet with you\n
around, Ukon. Nothing too out of the ordinary's\n
happened.
Guess I'll try the other neighborhoods.\n
I'm looking for something to really leave\n
an impression.
Now that I think on it... I suppose those\n
folks in the tenant houses HAVE been on-edge.
Oh, yeah. Definitely smells like something\n
fishy is going on down there.
Some pretty wealthy-looking folk going in and\n
out at all hours, talking nonsense...
Oh? Now that's interesting.
The longer we hang around, the more old women\n
seem to emerge from the woodwork, hearing\n
Ukon's name.
Soon enough, they begin to chat up a storm\n
about their husbands' shortcomings, local\n
rumors...
Little girls--undoubtedly their children--\n
flock about Ukon's feet and tug on him,\n
begging to play.
Guess that's Oshtor for you. Charming the\n
ladies no matter their age.
...So why do I get stuck with the old men\n
giving me snacks?
Thanks. All right, kid, let's get moving along.
Hrmf?
Finally. Listening to old women talk takes\n
forever no matter where you are, I guess.
Here, try these. The mamutu and the dango are\n
both pretty good.
Ah, thanks.
Was that really OK, back there? All I did\n
was chat them up and eat their food.
Yep, you did perfect. Just keep it up for\n
the next place we hit.
I guess that's easy enough...
All right, c'mon. Next stop.
Yo! Anyone home?
Ukon calls into the run-down tenant home...
...And after a moment, a gaggle of scruffy-\n
looking men emerge, giving off a shady vibe.
Tenant
Wheh? Who the--Oh! If it ain't Ukon.
Haven't seen you 'round these parts in a while.\n
Thought you'd up and died on us.
Everything all right, chief? Been a long time\n
since we saw you.
Yeah, sorry about that. I had some stuff to\n
take care of.
Ukon raises his pinky, then sticks his hand\n
inside his shirt to mimic a bulging, pregnant\n
belly crudely.
I-I had no idea you had a girl tucked away\n
somewhere, geez!
Bahahaha! I'm just pulling your leg. I was out\n
of the capital on business, that's all.
Huh. These guys might look a little, uh,\n
questionable... but they don't seem to be bad\n
people.
My, my. Ukon! Babe. Honey. I've missed you SO\n
much. It's been far too long!
...A distressingly muscular man in women's\n
clothing emerges from the house, approaching\n
us.
What brings you around our little den of\n
iniquity? And who's the cutie you brought\n
with you?
Bwuh!?
The man(?) winks at me, and suddenly I feel\n
supremely uncomfortable.
I-I take it all back. This place is definitely\n
dangerous. Too dangerous.
Ah, this is a friend of mine from out of town.\n
You'll probably be seeing a lot more of him.
I'm Haku. It's a pleasure. Yeah, Ukon picked\n
me up on the road and now I'm getting the\n
grand tour.
He's a countryside kid, so no need to be all\n
stiff and formal with him.
Nah, nah! Wouldn't dream of disrespecting\n
a friend of yours, chief.
It wouldn't take much to brush up his looks,\n
y'know, Ukon. I could get him looking as cute\n
as you.
Gfrah!?
Every time that infernal wink is aimed my way,\n
I feel like my throat is sealing itself up.
Oh, babe, relax! Look at you, getting all\n
nervous. You're lucky you're just my type.
Eh? Getting on good terms with the locals\n
already, huh? Good for you, kid.
Wait, wh--How can you even interpret this\n
like that!?
Oh, but back to the serious stuff--You guys been\n
holding up OK? Nothing weird lately?
Nothing real unusual. You know how it is.\n
Brothels and gambling dens are doin' business\n
as ever.
Oh, but... Babe. I'd stay away from that new\n
place that just popped up, if I were you.
New place? Care to fill me in?
That old storehouse by the river. Used to just\n
be for shipping booze by canal boat, but...
The old owner's out of the picture now,\n
apparently. Some new crew has been using it\n
as a base.
Pretty aggressive folk. They do as they please\n
over there, and no one is really doin' much\n
about it.
Doesn't seem like anyone we know is running\n
the show, but they walk around here like they\n
own the town.
They pay well, so we kinda turn a blind eye to\n
the ruckus, but we oughta teach 'em some\n
respect.
Rumor's that they have some sorta bigwig\n
backing 'em. Nobles! Not a drop of honor in the\n
lot, pfeh.
That's... very interesting. Sorry, guys, but I'm\n
gonna have to ask you to leave this one to me.
Huh? A'ight, I guess. If you say so, chief.
Much appreciated. What else do you know about\n
these guys?
OK. Leave the rest to me. Don't do anything\n
stupid in the meantime, all right?
Gotcha. And thanks, chief. We can always count\n
on you for stuff like this.
Come back any time. Door's always open for you.
You too, young man. If you ever come by the\n
brothel, I can take GOOD care of a cutie like\n
you.
I don't know why, but I'm sweating like crazy\n
even though it's not even hot out...
Seems you're well-liked around here.
Think so? It makes me happy to hear that,\n
thanks.
This was exactly how my old man used to do\n
things.
I see...
You oughta be just fine. They're a bunch of\n
moody guys, yeah, but you seemed to get along\n
perfectly.
Life's full of surprises, I guess.\n
You'll be fast friends soon enough.
I might need to reconsider that one...
Our patrol stretches on, and as we finally\n
start to wrap up, the sun dips low in the\n
orange sky.
Guess that's good enough for now. I think I've\n
got a general lay of the land.
You're talking about that storehouse?
Yeah. Judging by what we heard today, that's\n
gotta be our mark.
You told those guys to "leave it to you,"\n
but what exactly do you intend to do?
Can't quite say for certain. Not yet, anyway.\n
I'll look into it a little more before I make\n
plans.
Well, just... don't do anything reckless over it.
You've helped me out a lot. I'd prefer not\n
risking my life, but if you need my help, I'm\n
right here.
Glad to hear it. Good to know I can count on\n
you, kid.
Kinda strange, don't you think...?\n
For some reason, it feels right, this.\n
Just walkin' beside you.
Ukon grins widely.
It's an energetic, genuine smile--the kind that\n
can charm courtly nobles and commoners alike.
I decided to hit up the dining hall for a snack,\n
but it looks like someone had the same idea.
What was her name... Atuy, wasn't it?
Oh, hullo. The disappointing guy.\n
I haven't seen you since that one time.
Who's disappointing, now?
It's the girl I guided to the Hakurokaku Inn\n
the other day... The one with no filter.
You here to grab lunch?
Oh, it's not for me. I'm just here to feed\n
this little fellow.
Atuy indicates the hat on her head.
Your hat...? Your head?
Hat? Oh, hee hee. Yeah, I guess that's a\n
pretty common mistake.
This little one is Kurarin.
...Kurarin?\n
What's a Kurarin?
Kurarin is Kurarin!
Atuy pats the Kurarin(???) on her head fondly...
...which jiggles enthusiastically, as though\n
responding.
It's... alive?
Of course! Kurarin's a proud denizen of the\n
sea, after all.
Now that she mentions it... It does look\n
slimy and lifelike. Not like cloth or leather\n
at all.
We really don't quite understand Kurarin.\n
They just sort of drift around the beaches,\n
yeah?
They go where the wind takes them, eat anything,\n
and they're gentle as long as we leave them be.
I'm surprised you'd wear something that\n
mysterious as, uh... as a hat.
Hee hee. This little guy's no trouble. Seems to\n
like riding up there and wouldn't hurt a fly,\n
so--
Atuy takes the creature from her head and talks\n
lovingly to it, holding it in front of her\n
chest.
Kurarin, say hullo.
Then, it floats out of Atuy's grip and toward\n
me, waving a tentacle as though in greeting.
Whoa, it floats?
Yeah, I just said, didn't I?
Well, when you said floating, I figured you\n
meant, like--floating in the water. At sea.
It's really alive, huh...
You don't get them around here? They're pretty\n
common along the coast...
No, I've never seen one.
The sight of this translucent, wobbling mass\n
in the air... It's like I'm watching a magic\n
trick.
It obeyed Atuy's commands, so it must have a\n
pretty good amount of intelligence.
Hey, isn't it a cutie, though?
...Yeah, it's not that cute.
You said you were here to feed it, right?\n
What does it eat?
You said it eats up anything, but what about,\n
like... people? Does it scavenge for carrion,\n
or...
I pull back my hand cautiously, asking Atuy\n
about my sudden concerns.
Oh, no, nothing like that. A young one like\n
this? Insects, fish, maybe some fresh meat\n
would do the trick.
It'll get playful and shock people sometimes,\n
but it doesn't mean any harm.
...Shock?
Yeah, with this little tentacle here.
Atuy points at one of the dangling appendages\n
hanging from Kurarin's side.
Yikes. I almost touched that.
Oh, don't be so scared! It's just a little\n
baby shock.
Sure...
Going by what I've experienced so far, taking\n
people at their word for this stuff never ends\n
well.
*Poke, poke*
Kurarin pokes Atuy's shoulder.
Hm? Yeah, it IS taking a while.
Something wrong?
Yeah, it's just that it's been a while since\n
I asked for Kurarin's food. Maybe there's a\n
problem...?
No choice, I s'pose. I'll duck in and see if\n
everything's all right.
Look after this little one, won't you, love?
Huh? H-Hey--
With that, Atuy leaves the dining hall in a\n
hurry.
...She asked me to look after it, but what\n
exactly am I... supposed to do?
As instructed, I watch over Kurarin... which\n
gracefully floats about, distinctly lacking\n
cuteness.
This is "cute," huh.
No matter how I look at it, I just can't get\n
"cute" out of this. It's not like it has a\n
charming face.
...Charm, huh.
I take a writing brush from my pocket and set\n
it against the creature's slimy skin.
Let's draw one up, here... There you go.\n
Maybe you'll be cuter with a face.
You never know. You could be more popular with\n
the girls this way. Ladykiller Kurarin.
Two quick circles, a half-moon crescent for a\n
mouth, and we're golden.
...Weird. That was supposed to make you cute,\n
not even weirder.
...Maybe if I add the pupils, too...?
...OK, now we've crossed the line into\n
"creepy."
This thing is gonna haunt my nightmares if\n
I don't erase it.
I gently take Kurarin and wipe its face(???)\n
with a hand towel.
Ngh, slippery. Hard to wipe--Gah!
*Slip*
Ah, Haku. So this is where you got off to.\n
Dear sister was--
*Stick*
EEP!?
Kurarin adheres to Nekone's cheek, and she\n
flings the creature away with all the strength\n
she has.
I understand how she feels, but c'mon, that's\n
just cruel.
Wh-Wh-Wh-What is that!?
It's Kurarin.
And a Kurarin is WHAT, pray tell?
Kurarin is Kurarin.
Well, uh, how should I put this. I was...\n
trying to make it cuter? But it's hard to\n
wipe off, so--
And that is your notion of "cute"? I will be\n
seeing that horror in my dreams tonight.
Don't say that. I didn't think it would turn\n
out like this!
It would be a disaster if you let the stuff of\n
nightmares out in public.
"Disaster" is stretching it a little...
Anyway. Dear sister is looking for you. And now\n
that I have delivered the message, I will take\n
my leave.
With that, Nekone quickly exits the dining\n
hall.
Huh?
...And as my attention returns to the room,\n
I realize I don't see the flung-away Kurarin\n
anywhere.
GAH!?
EEK!
Geh!? S-So you've shown yourself, you monster!
Then--faintly muffled--I can hear the sound of\n
someone falling down stairs, breaking plates,\n
yelling...
Oof. S-Sorry about that.
Oh, for shame, love! I thought I asked you to\n
keep an eye on it.
Sorry, sorry. I only took my eyes away for a\n
second...
Oh, but aren't you lucky, Kurarin?\n
Getting such pretty makeup done!
*Jiggle, jiggle, jiggle*
Hee. Kurarin's so happy.
Kurarin wraps its tentacles around my face with\n
every indication of eager affection...
And the nightmarish face begins to emit an\n
eerie light.
BLRGLRGHLRGLRGH--
Aw! Hee, you see? So happy! That's a thanks\n
for doing such a good job with the makeover.
BLGLRGHRLGH--
H-How is this a "tiny little baby shock"!?\n
Buh--
As I slip into unconsciousness, I make a note\n
not to trust anyone else's sense of scale\n
around here.
*WHUMP*
Agh--
Finally freed from my desk work, I lean back\n
to stretch, only to bash my arm against the\n
table's corner.
Ow, ow... Tch. Grazed it.
I roll up my sleeve to find blood oozing from\n
the scratch, grimacing.
I should tell Kuon... Eh, no, I shouldn't\n
bother her. This'll heal up on its own.
If it were a more serious wound, maybe, but\n
I'd feel bad bothering her over a tiny scratch.
*Grind, grind, grind, grind*
Grinding noises echo from behind Kuon's door\n
as I pass by...
What's that sound?
Hm hm-hm...
And she's... humming to herself?
I decide to peek into her room, and a strange\n
fragrance wafts across my nose--and I\n
understand.
Ah, I see. She's working...
What are you doing, hovering out there?\n
C'mon, why don't you come in?
Oh, just being curious. I didn't want to\n
interrupt, sorry.
I see. I'll be done soon, so just hang around\n
for a bit.
She indicates a cushion, and I take a seat,\n
watching her work.
Kuon grinds something dark green and paste-like\n
into a mortar, continuing to hum pleasantly.
...So I, uh, assume you're making medicine\n
there? I mean, I doubt you're grinding up\n
condiments.
This? This'll be a new salve, I think. I wanted\n
to put some rare herbs I found to proper use.
That's impressive. You're always prepared,\n
huh...?
Looking around her room, there's a lot more\n
equipment in here than when we first moved\n
in...
...all of it inscrutable to the untrained eye,\n
its uses unclear at best.
They're probably tools for her apothecary work,\n
but to me they're not much more than\n
curiosities.
Confused? Anything in here look curious to you?
Yeah, the equipment looks curious, but...\n
What sort of effect does this weird-looking\n
fruit have?
I mean, I understand that it's probably an\n
ingredient or reagent of some kind, but...
I gingerly pick up a poisonous-looking,\n
reddish-black fruit replete with yellow spots.
That's an ikoraship. Ingested, it has an\n
antipyretic effect--lowers body temperature,\n
I should say.
Ingest? If I saw this laying in the forest,\n
I'd stay away. It looks poisonous.
Even if I knew its effects, I don't think I'd\n
want to eat it.
That's why I grind it up in a mortar. It's\n
easier to take as a pill or a powder.
And that way, I can add analgesics like tenma\n
or piwayo, which makes it a really effective\n
cold medicine.
It's got a fatal flaw, though--it's so bitter,\n
even a stunning beauty would make a sour face\n
after tasting it.
That's... sort of understandable, but sort of\n
not? That doesn't make me want to put it in my\n
mouth.
The best medicines are always the ones with\n
the most bitter taste.
I'll be really careful not to catch a cold\n
around you, then.
Ahaha. That's probably for the best. No better\n
medicine than not getting sick in the first\n
place.
You really are knowledgeable, though. Do you\n
know the names and effects of everything in\n
here?
Mhm. My mother was my instructor when I was\n
just an apprentice apothecary.
She was normally very gentle and kind, but she\n
became strict when she taught me.
She'd hit me if I made a mistake, and\n
oftentimes, we'd study through the night and\n
into the morning.
Sounds like a pretty stern woman to have for\n
a teacher.
Mm, well--she had to be. There are a lot of\n
dangerous substances involved in apothecary\n
work.
Even a small error when measuring dosage can\n
cause serious trouble, so you have to be\n
meticulous.
Serious trouble?
Medicines in high concentrations might as well\n
be toxins. They can disable or even kill a\n
person.
Kill a person... Yeah, I can see why she was\n
so strict with your education, then.
Yep. There's no such thing as being too strict\n
when you've got people's lives in your hands.
I understand that much, but didn't your mother\n
being so overbearing... I dunno, get to you at\n
all?
Did you ever just rebel and go "I don't want\n
to be an apothecary?"
Hm. I mean, it's not as though I never had\n
thoughts like that, but...
Kuon puts down her tools and looks up at the\n
ceiling, as though reflecting on her memories.
She had a proper reason for being so tough\n
about medicine, and otherwise, she was a warm\n
I remember whenever I got sick, she'd stay by\n
my bedside and take care of me...
And when I finally started making medicine\n
properly myself, she was ecstatic.
...Though I can't say I'm a huge fan of how she\n
made me take bitter medicine "for my health."
Even so, it must be a good memory--Kuon is\n
smiling with nostalgia.
But just for a moment. A shadow of loneliness\n
seems to creep into her smile.
Kuon?
...Ah. It's nothing.
Shaking her head, she returns to grinding with\n
her mortar and pestle.
Unfamiliar liquids and powders go into the\n
deep-green mix from earlier...
Mm, that should just about do it.\n
Here, Haku.
The salve-like contents of the mortar go into\n
a shell container, which she hands to me.
The salve you just made...\n
You're giving it to me?
You always seem to be getting cuts and scrapes,\n
so you should hold onto a fresh supply, I think.
I'll make more again when you run out, so don't\n
be stingy with it.
I see. I'll use it well, then.\n
Thank you.
That said... This stuff won't make me all itchy\n
like last time, will it? I'd rather not revisit\n
that...
Well, I should get going. I've got some work to\n
take care of.
Hold on a moment.
Kuon grabs my sleeve as I turn to go.
Show me your arm, Haku.
She rolls up my sleeve, exposing my arm--\n
or rather, the scrape I'd acquired earlier.
Wh-What? I'm surprised you noticed that.\n
It's just a scrape...
It's not worth bothering an apothecary over.\n
I'll just let it heal up naturally, so...
Nope. That won't fly with me. Have a seat, you.
Despite my objections, Kuon pushes me back to\n
her workstation and makes me sit.
Better to be safe than sorry. Even small wounds\n
can become problems if they fester or get\n
infected.
Apothecary wisdom?
That's right. I'll put an antiseptic on it,\n
so hold still.
No matter what I say, I don't think I'm\n
worming my way out of this one...
Hey, uh... If you can avoid it, I'd really\n
prefer a salve that doesn't itch so much.
Ah, this one won't itch, I think.
Kuon gives me a wide, toothy smile.\n
I think I've seen that one before.
Really? So I'll be all right even if it's\n
totally slathered on?
Well, that I can't guarantee.
Huh?
Disregarding my confusion, Kuon scrapes the\n
last of the mortar's contents out and pastes\n
them on.
Ack...! Ow, ow, it stings--!!
What sort of medicine is this? It doesn't just\n
sting--it hurts!
Sharp prickling sensations assault the irritated\n
parts of the wound...
It stings, then? I figured it might.\n
It's effective, but its drawback is it\n
irritates a little.
What do you mean, "drawback?" Ow, ow...\n
Wh-Why don't you improve the formula or\n
something?
I told you before, the best medicine is always\n
bitter. If it has faults, you know it will\n
I-I hear what you're saying, but... Gah.\n
I'd prefer something that helps more than \n
it hurts!
All right, bear with me and let me bandage it.\n
You shouldn't rush this--your body's important,\n
y'know.
I guess that's true, but...
Point taken. I trail off and let Kuon go about\n
her treatment.
...Ow ow ow.
A prickling pain shoots up my arm as she wraps\n
a bandage around the wound.
A-Are you almost done?
My body tenses up at the strange, exotic pain,\n
altogether different from the pain of the\n
scrape...
That should do it. Next time you get hurt,\n
don't write it off like it's no big deal.\n
Come see me.
U-Understood.
I can't help but nod as she projects a\n
professional, doctor-like aura.
